title: Resource implementation guidance avoids redundant lifecycle logging by default
description: A synthetic implementation-guidance scenario where a contributor is adding provider-side CRUD logging such as `Creating`, `Reading`, `Updating`, or `Deleting` to a new typed resource. The correct guidance should treat those logs as redundant by default, preserve the narrow remove-from-state diagnostic exception, and prefer SDK-level consistency if lifecycle logging is desired broadly.

scope.notes: Synthetic implementation scenario focused on suppressing redundant lifecycle logging in generated/provider-authored code examples and guidance.

mustCatch.description: The guidance should say generic `Import check`, `Creating`, `Reading`, `Updating`, and `Deleting` logs are redundant by default when they only duplicate Terraform core or provider-native logging.
The guidance should preserve the narrow remove-from-state/not-found diagnostic exception when it adds distinct debugging value.

mustNotFlag.description: The guidance must not present generic CRUD lifecycle/provider logging as a required or recommended default implementation pattern.

mustHaveSections: minimal set of code changes needed

notes: Use this case to benchmark the resource-implementation skill against the maintainer decision that generic lifecycle/provider logging is redundant by default.
